description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a striking red and dark brown color scheme, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings supported by black struts. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the tail, which displays a checkerboard pattern and a cross insignia on the vertical stabilizer. The front of the airplane is equipped with two large, exposed piston engines, each with a wooden propeller, and the nose is adorned with a flame motif. The cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing, likely open, and appears to be a single-seat configuration. The landing gear is not fully visible but hints at a taildragger setup, typical for classic biplanes. The overall design suggests a robust, maneuverable aircraft reminiscent of early 20th-century military planes.
description: A vintage red and brown biplane with dual exposed engines, wooden propellers, and a checkerboard tail, evoking early military aircraft. The cockpit is open and set behind the upper wing, with bold insignia and flame details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual exposed engines, wooden propellers, and the symmetrical biplane wing structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the checkerboard tail, cross insignia, and the flame motif on the nose.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail structure, vertical stabilizer, and the alignment of the wings and fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same checkerboard and cross details, with a clear look at the wing struts and engine placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings mounted one above the other, supported by vertical struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin exposed piston engines mounted on the lower wings, each with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; features a checkerboard pattern and cross insignia.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with main gear under the wings and a small tail wheel; gear appears fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Cockpit is forward-mounted just behind the upper wing, open configuration, likely single-seat with minimal canopy.
